proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one binary feature among feature_001..feature_127 has a non-zero mean difference in pfs_months between its 0/1 levels (univariate scan).
Mean pfs_months is higher in patients with feature_042=1 than feature_042=0 (positive main effect, plausible treatment).
Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with feature_056=1 than feature_056=0 (negative main effect, plausible adverse marker).

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df[c]==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df[c]==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-tests across all 82 binary features. Largest signed mean differences: feature_056 -1.54 mo (p<1e-300); feature_042 +1.10 mo (p<1e-300); feature_048 -1.05 mo (p=1.5e-178); feature_111 +0.57 mo (p=3.7e-143); feature_015 -0.56 mo (p=6.1e-144); feature_040 -0.46 mo (p=6.1e-70); feature_039 +0.36 mo (p=5.8e-59); feature_034 +0.11 mo (p=1.0e-6). All other binary features had |diff|<0.15 mo.
feature_042=1 group: mean pfs_months 5.41; feature_042=0 group: 4.31. Difference +1.098 mo (Welch t, p<1e-300).
feature_056=1: mean pfs_months 3.62; feature_056=0: 5.15. Difference -1.536 mo (Welch t, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months increases with feature_080 (continuous feature, mean 65, range 30-90, plausibly age or a strongly prognostic biomarker).
Mean pfs_months decreases as feature_067 (continuous) increases.
Mean pfs_months decreases as feature_101 (continuous) increases.
Mean pfs_months increases as feature_019 (continuous, range 1.6-5.5) increases.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.702 between feature_080 and pfs_months (p<1e-300). Decile means rise monotonically from 1.70 mo (lowest decile) to 7.84 mo (highest decile). OLS slope +0.176 mo per unit of feature_080.
Pearson r=-0.116 between feature_067 and pfs_months (p=4.1e-148). OLS slope -0.075 mo per unit.
Pearson r=-0.091 between feature_101 and pfs_months (p=3.0e-93). OLS slope -0.019 mo per unit.
Pearson r=+0.100 between feature_019 and pfs_months (p=9.4e-112). OLS slope +0.499 mo per unit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across feature_011 (race) groups (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across feature_089 (insurance) groups (private, medicare, medicaid, uninsured).
Mean pfs_months declines monotonically with feature_063 (3-level ordinal, plausibly stage or grade).
Mean pfs_months differs across the other multi-level integer features (feature_021, feature_024, feature_016, feature_127, feature_001, feature_073).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_011: white 4.679, black 4.717, hispanic 4.707, asian 4.735, other 4.762. One-way ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.494. No racial differences in PFS detected.
Mean pfs_months by feature_089: private 4.685, medicare 4.692, medicaid 4.706, uninsured 4.733. One-way ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.818. No insurance differences in PFS detected.

Mean pfs_months by feature_063: level 0 = 5.640 (n=17592); level 1 = 4.444 (n=24971); level 2 = 3.289 (n=7437). Strict monotone decline. Pearson r=-0.320 (p<1e-300). Linear slope -1.18 mo per increment.

feature_021 r=+0.004 (p=0.34); feature_024 r=+0.0002 (p=0.96); feature_016 r=-0.002 (p=0.72); feature_127 r=+0.005 (p=0.30); feature_001 r=-0.004 (p=0.38); feature_073 r=-0.001 (p=0.86). None of the other ordinal/multi-level integer features showed any association with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment for feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_015, feature_040, feature_039, feature_034, feature_067, feature_101, feature_019, both feature_042 and feature_111 retain independent positive effects on pfs_months.
feature_039 and feature_034 lose their univariate signal once feature_042/feature_111 and other major prognostic features are included (i.e., they are confounded).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_111 + feature_042 + feature_015 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_034 + feature_067 + feature_101 + feature_019', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.792. Stable independent coefficients: feature_080 +0.176/unit (t=343); feature_063 -1.17/unit (t=-156); feature_056 -1.55 (t=-139); feature_042 +1.10 (t=103); feature_048 -0.98 (t=-58); feature_015 -0.58 (t=-54); feature_111 +0.53 (t=36); feature_040 -0.47 (t=-33); feature_019 +0.48/unit (t=47); feature_067 -0.077/unit (t=-58); feature_101 -0.019/unit (t=-45). feature_039 (+0.014, p=0.32) and feature_034 (+0.002, p=0.88) lost significance after adjustment, indicating their univariate signals were confounded.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_042 is modified by feature_111 (positive interaction): patients with both feature_042=1 and feature_111=1 have longer PFS than the additive prediction.
The PFS benefit of feature_042 is abolished or reversed in patients with feature_015=1 (negative interaction).
The PFS benefit of feature_042 is abolished in patients with feature_040=1 (negative interaction).
The PFS benefit of feature_111 is abolished in patients with feature_015=1 or feature_040=1 (analogous resistance).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_042 * feature_111', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_111 interaction beta = +1.583, p<1e-300. Main effects of feature_042 alone (-0.004, p=0.79) and feature_111 alone (+0.004, p=0.79) are essentially zero after the interaction is included. Strong supra-additive synergy.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_042 * feature_015', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_015 interaction beta = -1.673, p<1e-300; main feature_042 +1.681 in feature_015=0 patients, neutralized when feature_015=1.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_042 * feature_040', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_040 interaction beta = -1.380, p<1e-300; main feature_042 +1.358 in feature_040=0 patients, neutralized when feature_040=1.
feature_111:feature_015 interaction beta = -0.839, p=2.5e-156; feature_111:feature_040 interaction beta = -0.648, p=6.3e-61. Both feature_015 and feature_040 also abolish feature_111's main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 and feature_111 are independently distributed (their co-occurrence is the chance product of their marginals), implying the synergy is biological/pharmacological rather than a treatment-pattern artifact.
Patients with feature_042=1 AND feature_111=1 have substantially longer PFS than patients with neither, while patients with only one of the two have PFS indistinguishable from neither.
feature_042 receipt does not differ across feature_011 (race) or feature_089 (insurance) groups.
feature_111 receipt does not differ across feature_011 (race) or feature_089 (insurance) groups.

analyses.result_summary: 2x2 table feature_042 x feature_111: chi-square = 0.47, p=0.492. Joint frequencies (0.198, 0.454, 0.105, 0.244) match marginal product within rounding. Independent assignment.
Mean pfs_months by 042 x 111 cells: (0,0) 4.30 (n=9880); (0,1) 4.31 (n=22692); (1,0) 4.29 (n=5234); (1,1) 5.89 (n=12194). Combination uniquely better; either drug alone confers no benefit.

feature_042=1 prevalence by feature_011: white 0.348, black 0.351, hispanic 0.351, asian 0.345, other 0.337. Chi-square p=0.870. By feature_089: private 0.348, medicare 0.350, medicaid 0.349, uninsured 0.346. Chi-square p=0.969. No demographic disparity in feature_042 receipt.

feature_111=1 prevalence by feature_011: white 0.697, black 0.700, hispanic 0.696, asian 0.708, other 0.687. Chi-square p=0.644. By feature_089: private 0.694, medicare 0.701, medicaid 0.700, uninsured 0.694. Chi-square p=0.529. No demographic disparity in feature_111 receipt.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combination benefit (feature_042=1 AND feature_111=1) is restricted to patients with feature_015=0 (i.e., feature_015 is a binary resistance marker that fully abolishes the synergy).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_042 * feature_111 * feature_015', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Three-way feature_042:feature_111:feature_015 = -2.357 (p<1e-300). feature_042:feature_111 in feature_015=0 stratum = +2.387 (p<1e-300). Within feature_015=0: combo cell mean 6.68 vs 4.30 baseline (diff +2.38). Within feature_015=1: combo cell mean 4.36 vs 4.28 baseline (no benefit).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combination benefit (feature_042=1 AND feature_111=1) is restricted to patients with feature_040=0 (i.e., feature_040 is a second binary resistance marker that fully abolishes the synergy).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_042 * feature_111 * feature_040', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Three-way feature_042:feature_111:feature_040 = -1.888 (p<1e-300). feature_042:feature_111 in feature_040=0 stratum = +1.926 (p<1e-300). Within feature_040=0: combo cell mean 6.24 vs 4.30 baseline. Within feature_040=1: combo cell mean 4.34 vs 4.32 baseline (no benefit).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 82 binary features, only feature_015 and feature_040 are major modifiers of the combo (feature_042 AND feature_111) effect; other features show weaker or no modification.
feature_034 (binary, prev 0.37) appears as a modifier of the combo effect because it is a positive sensitizer; the combo benefit is larger when feature_034=1.
feature_101 (continuous) interacts negatively with the combo: each unit increase reduces the combo benefit.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + _combo * '+c, data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Top binary modifiers of _combo (=feature_042*feature_111): feature_015 -2.371 (p<1e-300), feature_040 -1.931 (p<1e-300), feature_034 +0.535 (p=8.1e-67). All other binary features had |interaction| < 0.30 mo and p > 5e-4. feature_015 and feature_040 are the dominant resistance markers.
_combo:feature_101 interaction = -0.074 mo per unit (p<1e-300). feature_101 has both a small adverse main effect and a stronger negative effect specifically on the combo benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The apparent feature_034 sensitization of the combo is driven by feature_015/feature_040 distribution (feature_034 may simply correlate with absence of resistance markers); once feature_015 and feature_040 are conditioned on, feature_034 has no effect on the combo benefit.

An omnibus model with feature_042 * feature_111 * feature_015 and feature_042 * feature_111 * feature_040, plus core prognostic variables, explains the bulk of variance in pfs_months and the additional feature_034 term is non-significant.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months in (combo=1, sensitive=1) cells: feature_034=0 7.236 (n=3552), feature_034=1 7.177 (n=3019). Difference -0.06 mo, not meaningful. feature_034's apparent combo modification is fully explained by its (modest) correlation with the absence of resistance markers.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_067 + feature_101 + feature_019 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_034 + feature_042 * feature_111 * feature_015 + feature_042 * feature_111 * feature_040', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Omnibus model R^2=0.865 (adj 0.865). feature_042:feature_111 = +2.737 (p<1e-300). feature_042:feature_111:feature_015 = -2.395 (p<1e-300). feature_042:feature_111:feature_040 = -1.892 (p<1e-300). feature_034 coefficient -0.006 (p=0.50), confirming it has no independent effect once resistance markers are modelled.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 and feature_040 act as alternative resistance mechanisms: presence of either is sufficient to abolish the combo benefit (logical OR), and there is no further loss when both are present.

analyses.result_summary: Combo benefit by resistance status: feature_015=0 & feature_040=0: combo PFS 7.21 vs no-combo 4.30, diff +2.91 (p<1e-300, n=26760). feature_015=0 & feature_040=1: diff -0.01 (p=0.91, n=5875). feature_015=1 & feature_040=0: diff +0.04 (p=0.37, n=14180). feature_015=1 & feature_040=1: diff +0.10 (p=0.29, n=3185). Either marker alone is sufficient to abolish benefit; double-positive is not worse than single-positive (no epistasis).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_042 is not predicted by feature_011 (race), feature_089 (insurance), feature_080 (age), feature_063 (stage), or any of the resistance/prognostic markers.
Receipt of feature_111 likewise is not predicted by demographic or prognostic features.
Distribution of feature_063 (stage) does not differ across feature_011 (race) or feature_089 (insurance) groups.

analyses.code: smf.logit('feature_042 ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_011 + feature_089 + feature_015 + feature_040 + feature_034 + feature_056 + feature_048', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression: no covariate is significant. feature_080 OR per year 1.00 (p=0.71); feature_063 OR per increment 1.02 (p=0.14); each feature_011 dummy p>0.55; each feature_089 dummy p>0.78; feature_015 p=0.56; feature_040 p=0.22. Treatment receipt of feature_042 appears effectively random with respect to all observed features.

Logistic regression for feature_111: no covariate significant. feature_011 dummies p>0.20; feature_089 dummies p>0.37; feature_080 p=0.80; feature_063 p=0.38; feature_015 p=0.71; feature_040 p=0.67. Treatment receipt of feature_111 appears effectively random.
feature_063 distribution by feature_011: chi-square p=0.892. By feature_089: chi-square p=0.690. No disparity in disease severity by race or insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the molecular-sensitive subgroup (feature_015=0 AND feature_040=0), the combo (feature_042 AND feature_111) PFS benefit is homogeneous across feature_011 (race) groups.
Within the sensitive subgroup, the combo benefit is homogeneous across feature_089 (insurance) groups.
Within the sensitive subgroup, the combo benefit is homogeneous across tertiles of feature_080 (age).

analyses.result_summary: Combo - no-combo PFS difference (sensitive subgroup) by race: white +2.888 (p<1e-300, n=17437), black +2.948 (p=1.3e-95, n=3176), hispanic +2.931 (p=1.9e-130, n=4033), asian +3.026 (p=9.4e-53, n=1576), other +2.907 (p=1.3e-16, n=538). Differences across groups <0.15 mo, statistically indistinguishable.

Combo - no-combo PFS difference (sensitive subgroup) by insurance: private +2.938 (p<1e-300), medicare +2.868 (p<1e-300), medicaid +2.999 (p=5.5e-123), uninsured +2.770 (p=3.4e-33). All within 0.25 mo of each other, statistically indistinguishable.

Combo - no-combo PFS difference (sensitive subgroup) by feature_080 tertile: young +2.945 (p<1e-300), mid +2.829 (p<1e-300), old +2.962 (p<1e-300). Combo benefit is independent of age. (Interaction _combo:feature_080 in sensitive subgroup beta=+0.0004, p=0.87.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 is highly correlated with feature_111 (e.g., the two binary indicators co-occur in most patients) so its univariate signal is largely a proxy for feature_111; once feature_111 is conditioned on, feature_039 contributes no independent effect.

analyses.result_summary: feature_039 vs feature_111 cross-tab: 85.0% of patients have matching values; phi=0.669, chi-square p<1e-300. In a 3-way model feature_042*feature_111*feature_039, the feature_039 main effect (-0.028, p=0.49), feature_042:feature_039 interaction (+0.032, p=0.64), feature_111:feature_039 (+0.032, p=0.50), and 3-way (+0.041, p=0.62) are all non-significant; feature_042:feature_111 stays at +1.526 (p<1e-300). feature_039 is essentially a noisy proxy for feature_111.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the sensitive subgroup, feature_101 (continuous) acts as a continuous resistance gradient: each unit reduces the combo benefit by ~0.14 mo, even though its main effect is small.
Within the sensitive subgroup, feature_019 has a positive main effect on PFS but does not interact with the combo (i.e., its effect is additive, not modifying).

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_015=0 & feature_040=0: combo intercept benefit at feature_101=0 is +5.065 mo; each unit of feature_101 attenuates the benefit by -0.138 mo (p<1e-300). At feature_101 mean (~15.5) the combo benefit is ~+5.07 - 0.138*15.5 ≈ +2.93, matching the empirical mean. feature_101's main effect outside the combo is negligible (+0.0002, p=0.82).

Within sensitive subgroup: feature_019 main effect +0.494 mo per unit (p<1e-300, additive). _combo:feature_019 interaction = -0.081, p=0.052; not statistically significant. feature_019 contributes additively to PFS irrespective of combo treatment.
